help.title: Help Center
help.workflow.desc: Raw Questions - AI Standardization - Import - Generate Self-contained HTML
help.step1.desc: Follow the format specification, separate questions with ---
help.step2.desc: Use AI prompts to help convert non-standard format
help.step3.desc: Click import button or drag files into the window
help.step4.desc: Choose a theme and click generate for interactive HTML quizzes
help.ai_prompt_header: AI Prompt (click the button above to copy, then paste into AI with your quiz materials):
help.ai_prompt_zh:
Please convert the following questions (or questions in the image) into a standard txt question bank file, strictly following the formatting rules below:

[File Structure]
- The file may contain an optional title line: Title: Your Quiz Title (e.g., Title: Math Practice 1)
- Each question is separated by three dashes "---" on a single line
- Lines starting with # are treated as comments and will be ignored

[Question Format]
Each question must include:
Type: Single / Multiple / Fill / Judgment
Question: The text of the question
Options: Option1|Option2|Option3 (only for Single and Multiple choice, separated by |)
Answer: Depending on the question type (see below)

[Answer Format Details]
- Single choice: Fill in the index of the correct option, counting from 1. E.g., Answer: 2 means the second option is correct.
- Multiple choice: Fill in the indices of all correct options, separated by commas. E.g., Answer: 1,3,4
- Fill in the blank:
  - For a single blank: Multiple acceptable answers are separated by |. E.g., Answer: 42|forty-two
  - For multiple blanks: Separate blanks with semicolons (;). Each blank's answers are separated by |. E.g., Answer: 2|two;3|three
- Judgment: Answer must be "True" or "False" (or "Yes"/"No").

[Examples]
---
Type: Single
Question: What is 1+1?
Options: 1|2|3|4
Answer: 2

---
Type: Multiple
Question: Which are even numbers?
Options: 1|2|3|4
Answer: 2,4

---
Type: Fill
Question: Two plus two equals ?
Answer: 4|four

---
Type: Judgment
Question: The sky is blue.
Answer: True

[Notes]
- Output only the question text, no extra explanation or conversation.
- Option indexes start from 1.
- If the questions come from images or documents, convert them strictly.
- Keep the output as plain text, directly usable as a txt file.
